name: Vitoria-Gasteiz Green Belt Loop
description: Cycle the easy Anillo Verde de Vitoria-Gasteiz, a 17.3-mile route through diverse parks and natural landscapes around the city.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Vitoria-Gasteiz is characterized by an extensive network of primarily flat cycling paths, known as "bidegorris," which extend throughout the city and into its surrounding natural areas. The region features a significant Green Belt encircling the city, connecting various parks and natural spaces. Beyond the urban core, the Llanada Alavesa offers a flat landscape with numerous routes for cycle tourists, including converted old railway lines. The area provides a diverse environment for cycling, from urban greenways to reservoir perimeters and open plains. Best touring cycling routes around Vitoria-Gasteiz The most popular touring cycling route is Vitoria-Gasteiz Green Belt Loop, an easy 17.3 miles (27.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 45 minutes to complete. This route encircles the city, connecting several high-ecological-value parks. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Embalse Ullíbarri-Gamboa – circular desde Vitoria-Gasteiz, a moderate 29.9 miles (48.1 km) path. This route explores the perimeter of the Ullibarri-Gamboa Reservoir, passing through provincial parks like Garaio and Landa. Local touring cyclists also love the Vasco-Navarro Greenway and Estibaliz Sanctuary Loop, a 29.4 miles (47.3 km) trail leading through diverse landscapes along an old railway line, often completed in about 3 hours 12 minutes. Touring cycling around Vitoria-Gasteiz is defined by its extensive cycling network, the peri-urban Green Belt, and the scenic Vasco-Navarro Greenway. The region offers routes suitable for various ability levels, from easy urban loops to more challenging regional tours. The routes in Vitoria-Gasteiz are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 400 reviews. More than 6000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Vitoria-Gasteiz's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Vitoria-Gasteiz?
A: There are over 200 touring cycling routes available around Vitoria-Gasteiz, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a good mix, with over 40 easy routes, more than 70 moderate options, and over 120 challenging tours for experienced cyclists.

Q: Are there any easy, family-friendly cycling routes in Vitoria-Gasteiz?
A: Yes, Vitoria-Gasteiz is very family-friendly for cycling, thanks to its primarily flat terrain and extensive network of bike lanes. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1065719557/vitoria-gasteiz-green-belt-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vitoria-Gasteiz Green Belt Loop&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 27.8 km route that encircles the city, connecting several parks and natural spaces. The Vasco-Navarro Greenway is also known for being family-friendly.

Q: What are the best circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Vitoria-Gasteiz offers several excellent circular routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1065719557/vitoria-gasteiz-green-belt-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vitoria-Gasteiz Green Belt Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 30 km option encircling the city. For a longer ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1068562124/embalse-ullibarri-gamboa-circular-desde-vitoria-gasteiz" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse Ullíbarri-Gamboa – circular desde Vitoria-Gasteiz&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the perimeter of the Ullibarri-Gamboa Reservoir, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1069040216/vasco-navarro-greenway-and-estibaliz-sanctuary-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vasco-Navarro Greenway and Estibaliz Sanctuary Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the cycling routes?
A: Vitoria-Gasteiz's routes offer diverse natural beauty and landmarks. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1630139" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vasco-Navarro Railway Greenway&lt;/a&gt;, visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2989133" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Our Lady of Estíbaliz&lt;/a&gt;, or cycle around the Ullibarri-Gamboa Reservoir, passing through provincial parks like Garaio and Landa. The Green Belt itself connects high-ecological-value parks such as Salburua, known for its wetlands and birdwatching opportunities, and Armentia Forest.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking more challenge, the region offers over 120 difficult routes. While the city itself is largely flat, the nearby Montes de Vitoria provide higher elevations and more demanding terrain, featuring beech and kermes-oak forests. The wider Llanada Alavesa region also has longer routes like the 'Great Route through Llanada Alavesa' for extended adventures.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Vitoria-Gasteiz?
A: Vitoria-Gasteiz is enjoyable for touring cycling for much of the year. The spring and autumn months generally offer pleasant temperatures and lush landscapes, ideal for exploring the Green Belt and surrounding areas. Summer is also popular, especially for routes around the Ullibarri-Gamboa Reservoir where you can enjoy the water. The city's extensive cycling network makes it accessible in various seasons.

Q: Where can I find bike rentals near the cycling routes?
A: Bike rental options are available in Vitoria-Gasteiz, particularly at popular spots like Garaio Park, located by the Ullibarri-Gamboa Reservoir. This makes it convenient to access the reservoir's green route even if you don't bring your own bike.

Q: What makes Vitoria-Gasteiz a good destination for touring cyclists?
A: Vitoria-Gasteiz is an exceptional destination for touring cyclists due to its commitment to sustainability and its extensive, primarily flat cycling infrastructure. The city boasts 171 kilometers of bike lanes and a unique Green Belt encircling it. This, combined with the scenic Vasco-Navarro Greenway and routes around the Ullibarri-Gamboa Reservoir, offers a wide variety of well-maintained paths for all skill levels.

Q: Can I combine cycling with birdwatching or nature exploration?
A: Absolutely. The Salburua Wetlands, a key part of Vitoria-Gasteiz's Green Belt, are an internationally important wetland and a prime spot for birdwatching. You can cycle to and around this area, which features lagoons, wet meadows, and an Interpretation Center (Ataria) to learn about local flora and fauna. The Green Belt itself connects several high-ecological-value parks perfect for nature exploration.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Vitoria-Gasteiz?
A: The touring cycling routes around Vitoria-Gasteiz are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 400 reviews. More than 6000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the well-maintained paths, the accessibility of natural spaces, and the city's overall cyclist-friendly environment.

Q: Are there options for gravel cycling in the Vitoria-Gasteiz region?
A: Yes, Vitoria-Gasteiz is also a hub for gravel cycling. The region offers numerous trails, tracks, and low-traffic roads that are perfect for gravel bikes, providing a more adventurous cyclotourism experience beyond the paved bidegorris. This allows for exploration of the wider Llanada Alavesa and other natural areas.

Q: How can I access the Ullibarri-Gamboa Reservoir by bike from Vitoria-Gasteiz?
A: The Ullibarri-Gamboa Reservoir is easily accessed from Vitoria-Gasteiz via dedicated cycling lanes. Once there, you can enjoy a green route that allows you to tour almost its entire perimeter, passing through beautiful provincial parks like Garaio and Landa. Consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31901241" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vasco-Navarro Greenway – Ullíbarri-Gamboa Reservoir loop from Vitoria-Gasteiz&lt;/a&gt; for a comprehensive tour.
tips:
Located in the Green Belt of Vitoria-Gasteiz, to the south of the city, is the Olarizu Botanical Garden, created with the mission of "conserving, disseminating and enhancing local, regional, European and global plant biodiversity".

In its more than 120 hectares, the garden houses different collections of plants, including a representation of the main forests in Europe, as well as other plant formations, housing more than a thousand different species. It is an ideal place for a walk and learning about the world of plants.

The Atxa-Landaberde section is located to the north of the urban residential area of Ibaiondo, on the left bank of the Zadorra river, between Yurre and Gobeo. It owes its name to the place names that gave names to the hills where it is located, typical marly hills of the landscape of the Llanada Alavesa.

The Zabalgana park has a very special significance as it is the result of an intense restoration process, which has turned an area of old gravel pits, badly degraded, into a space of natural interest that is highly suitable for leisure and recreation for the population.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to cycle the Anillo Verde de Vitoria-Gasteiz – circular?
faqs.answer: Most cyclists complete the entire circular route in approximately 2.5 to 3 hours. This estimate accounts for a leisurely pace, allowing time to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Anillo Verde?
faqs.answer: The route features a varied terrain, primarily consisting of surfaced paths, pedestrian areas, and some roads. You'll also encounter sections of compacted gravel and minor unpaved segments, along with dedicated cycleways. It's generally flat with minimal elevation changes.

faqs.question: Is the Anillo Verde de Vitoria-Gasteiz suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Anillo Verde is considered an easy route, making it ideal for all fitness levels, including beginners and families. Its mostly paved surfaces and low elevation gain contribute to a comfortable cycling experience.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The route connects several peri-urban parks of high ecological value. You can explore areas like Olarizu Park, Zabalgana Park, and the Salburua Wetlands, where you might spot deer and various bird species. The trail offers a pleasant blend of natural tranquility and glimpses of the urban landscape.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the Anillo Verde?
faqs.answer: The Anillo Verde is enjoyable year-round, but spring and autumn offer the most pleasant cycling conditions with mild temperatures and beautiful natural colors. Summer is also popular, though it can be warmer, while winter cycling is possible but may require appropriate gear for cooler weather.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Anillo Verde de Vitoria-Gasteiz – circular trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally welcome on the Anillo Verde. However, it's important to keep them on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near other users, and to clean up after them to maintain the natural environment for everyone.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee to access the Anillo Verde?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits required or entrance fees to access the Anillo Verde de Vitoria-Gasteiz. It is a public green belt designed for everyone to enjoy freely.

faqs.question: Where can I park or access the Anillo Verde from Vitoria-Gasteiz?
faqs.answer: The Anillo Verde is highly accessible from any point in Vitoria-Gasteiz. There are eight suggested access routes from the city center. For parking, Atxa Park is one of the designated parking areas, and various street parking options are available around the city's perimeter parks.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see along the route?
faqs.answer: The Anillo Verde is rich in biodiversity. In areas like the Salburua Wetlands, you have a good chance of observing deer and a variety of bird species from dedicated observatories. The parks and natural corridors also host various smaller mammals and insects.

faqs.question: Does the Anillo Verde connect to other cycling routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, beyond its main loop, the Anillo Verde connects with other longer routes within the Green Itinerary Network of Álava. Notably, it integrates with sections of the Vuelta al Anillo Verde/Eraztun Berdearen bira and the Vasco-Navarroaren bide berdea (Vasco-Navarro Greenway), allowing for extended cycling tours into the region.

faqs.question: Are there facilities like cafes or restrooms along the Anillo Verde?
faqs.answer: While the Anillo Verde itself is a natural green belt, its proximity to Vitoria-Gasteiz means you'll find cafes, restaurants, and public restrooms easily accessible in the urban areas bordering the parks. It's advisable to carry water and snacks, especially for longer rides, as facilities might not be directly on the trail in all sections.
